explanation: The property states that in 'PSV Mode', at the 'end of an inspiration phase', if 'no inspiration pause is required' (interpreted as the inspiration pause button not being pressed) and the 'Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI', then a 'Recruitment Maneuver shall start' in the next state. This is a global implication where the combined trigger conditions lead to a next-state consequence. Template 11 'G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))' perfectly captures this. 'bool_exp2' represents all the trigger conditions joined by conjunction, and 'bool_exp3' represents the future state of the Recruitment Maneuver.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in PSV mode (PSVMode), the inspiratory phase has ended (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), no inspiratory pause is currently requested by the GUI (!inspiratoryPauseButton), and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) button is actively set by the GUI (RMButton) (together forming bool_exp1), then in the next state (X), the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) shall become active (bool_exp3). This aligns with the 'trigger implies next state' pattern of template 31.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode and the inspiratory phase ends, and simultaneously no inspiration pause is required (meaning the inspiratory pause button is not pressed) and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI, then in the next state (X), the Recruitment Maneuver shall start. This captures the conditional response requirement.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode and the inspiration phase ends, and simultaneously no inspiration pause is required and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI (all combined in bool_exp1), then in the very next state (X), the Recruitment Maneuver (RM, specified in bool_exp3) shall start. This captures the conditional trigger and the immediate future consequence.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode, the inspiratory phase ends, no inspiratory pause is required, and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) button is pressed (all combined into bool_exp1), then in the next state (X), a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) shall start (bool_exp3). This directly translates the given conditional requirement.

The property states that globally, whenever the inspiratory phase ends, and simultaneously no inspiration pause is required and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI, then a Recruitment Maneuver shall start immediately in the next state. This corresponds to a standard 'response' pattern where a specific combined trigger event leads to an immediate consequence.

The property states that globally (G), if it is 'PSV Mode' and 'the end of an inspiration phase' and 'no inspiration pause is required' and 'the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI', then in the next state (X), a 'Recruitment Maneuver' shall start. This is captured by a 'G (bool_exp1 -> (X bool_exp3))' template where bool_exp1 represents the combined antecedent conditions and bool_exp3 represents the consequent action in the next state.

The property describes a requirement within the 'PSV Mode'. When the 'inspiration phase ends' AND 'no inspiration pause is required' (i.e., inspiratoryPauseButton is false) AND a 'Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI' (RMButton is true), then a 'Recruitment Maneuver (RM) shall start' in the next state. This is captured by LTL template 41, which states 'Globally, if bool_exp1 AND bool_exp2 are true, then bool_exp3 will be true in the next state'. We assign 'PSVMode' to bool_exp1 as the general context. The specific trigger conditions, 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton', are assigned to bool_exp2. The consequence, 'RM', which indicates the Recruitment Maneuver being active, is assigned to bool_exp3, and preceded by the 'X' (Next) operator to signify it 'shall start' in the very next step.

The property states that in PSV mode, at the end of an inspiration phase, if an inspiration pause is not required and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set via the GUI, then the Recruitment Maneuver shall start in the very next state. This is captured by a 'globally, if trigger then next state consequent' pattern. 'bool_exp2' represents the combined triggering conditions (PSVMode active AND inspiration phase ends AND no inspiration pause set AND RM is set by GUI), and 'bool_exp3' represents the immediate start of the Recruitment Maneuver in the subsequent state.

The property states that globally, if the system is operating in PSV Mode (bool_exp1) and simultaneously the inspiratory phase ends, no inspiration pause is required, and the Recruitment Maneuver is set by the GUI (bool_exp2), then the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) (bool_exp3) shall start in the next state (X). This captures the conditional initiation of an RM under specific circumstances within PSV mode.
